name: James O'Keefe
subject: The White House responded
body:
Two weeks ago, my team released hidden-camera footage of a man on a Los Angeles sidewalk handing a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill.

| | | | | [](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z3bng3ti8ije08kougdlr4h7a5rg83uq0ll0rk88_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25")
---
| Dear Patriot,
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7gl7dt2matcuco4vn8djqf87iokcuog3dfk0adg_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25") Two weeks ago, my team released hidden-camera footage of a man on a Los Angeles sidewalk handing a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill. On the note was a name, an address, a date of birth. A real voter's identity. "Sign his name," the man said. "Just write what I tell you to write." **2.5 million people watched that video in the first 48 hours.** 82,000 liked it. 33,000 shared it. Eric Daugherty's post demanding "ARREST HIM NOW" hit 1.5 million views and 95,000 likes. Libs of TikTok's coverage: 846,000 views and 45,000 likes. The White House responded. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called our footage "astounding" and said the President is "working on an executive order to strengthen our elections." The DOJ issued a statement: they will "aggressively pursue anyone" involved in election fraud. Governor Newsom -- _Gavin Newsom_ -- said: "This alleged activity is a felony and should be prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law." Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman personally contacted us: "We have received your complaint and will open a file." **And then a detective from the Palm Beach County Sheriff 's Office showed up at my headquarters. Looking for me.** Not a single person from our videos has been arrested. But a law enforcement officer showed up at **my** door. **[▶ STAND WITH JAMES -- CHIP IN $35 TO KEEP US IN THE FIELD](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zfedea6jggikga5ovl04u3vdan7nha2de3e1ifmo_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25")** Here's what I need you to understand: Everything you just read -- the footage, the White House response, the DA opening a file, the Governor forced to respond -- all of it happened because of what we built together over the past four months. This year, O'Keefe Media Group investigations have generated over **9.9 million views on a single post.** We've triggered a **$9 billion Treasury audit.** We got **$253 million in federal contracts suspended.** A Secret Service agent was suspended within hours of our report. An FBI security specialist confessed on hidden camera that major fraud cases are deliberately slow-walked so "everyone walks." **That 's not opinion journalism. That's results.** But I'm going to be straight with you -- results cost money. And right now, I need to fund my team's operations through the entire month of April. We need to raise **$92,430** to cover fieldwork, equipment, legal defense, and payroll for the journalists who made every one of those investigations possible. That's the real number. That's what it takes. $35 covers a background check on the voter identities being stolen in Los Angeles. $50 funds a day of undercover equipment. $100 covers a full day of field operations. $250 goes directly toward the legal defense fund protecting our journalists from the people who want us silenced. **[▶ CHIP IN $50 TO FUND APRIL OPERATIONS](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zpkpfqrivuv0n6uecolu6kgits8qikh031hrmmrg_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25")** Bo Loudon, a supporter and political commentator, put it simply: "God bless James for uncovering this. These fraudsters NEED to be arrested." He's right. But arrests don't happen without evidence. Evidence doesn't happen without investigators. And investigators don't happen without you. We have four active investigations in the pipeline right now. Jonathan Choe, the journalist who broke the Los Angeles footage, is expanding into San Francisco's Tenderloin district. We're hiring new undercover operatives. The Citizen Justice League -- our network of trained citizen journalists -- is growing every week. But none of it moves forward without funding. Can I count on you for April? **[▶ YES -- RUSH $100 TO FUND APRIL INVESTIGATIONS](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zg8m4mnemfqi4b8o539f4j24gmpu8atb8dn8ongg_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25")** In Truth,
James O'Keefe
Founder and CEO
O'Keefe Media Group

**P.S.** 2.5 million people watched our footage. The White House responded. The DOJ promised action. The LA District Attorney opened a file. And a detective showed up at my door. All because of what your donations made possible. **Now I need $92,430 to fund April. Your $35, $50, or $100 today is the reason we can keep going when they try to make us stop.** **[Chip in now to fund April operations.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z57ifpc6op7rdgdmkn834msdmcfte06reh579li0_rp22sh2s8i6epr5cji60or4cjh2bs "https://secure.anedot.com/okeefemediagroup/omg-april-launch-hf?utm_source=omg-april-launch&utm_medium=hf&utm_campaign=20260411&utm_content=aan&amount=25")**

clean_body: Two weeks ago, my team released hidden-camera footage of a man on a Los Angeles sidewalk handing a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill. | | | | | "" | Dear Patriot, "" Two weeks ago, my team released hidden-camera footage of a man on a Los Angeles sidewalk handing a homeless person a post-it note and a dollar bill. On the note was a name, an address, a date of birth. A real voter's identity. "Sign his name," the man said. "Just write what I tell you to write." 2.5 million people watched that video in the first 48 hours. 82,000 liked it. 33,000 shared it. Eric Daugherty's post demanding "ARREST HIM NOW" hit 1.5 million views and 95,000 likes. Libs of TikTok's coverage: 846,000 views and 45,000 likes. The White House responded. Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt called our footage "astounding" and said the President is "working on an executive order to strengthen our elections." The DOJ issued a statement: they will "aggressively pursue anyone" involved in election fraud. Governor Newsom -- Gavin Newsom -- said: "This alleged activity is a felony and should be prosecuted to the fullest extent of the law." Los Angeles DA Nathan Hochman personally contacted us: "We have received your complaint and will open a file." And then a detective from the Palm Beach County Sheriff 's Office showed up at my headquarters. Looking for me. Not a single person from our videos has been arrested. But a law enforcement officer showed up at my door. "" Here's what I need you to understand: Everything you just read -- the footage, the White House response, the DA opening a file, the Governor forced to respond -- all of it happened because of what we built together over the past four months. This year, O'Keefe Media Group investigations have generated over 9.9 million views on a single post. We've triggered a $9 billion Treasury audit. We got $253 million in federal contracts suspended. A Secret Service agent was suspended within hours of our report. An FBI security specialist confessed on hidden camera that major fraud cases are deliberately slow-walked so "everyone walks." That 's not opinion journalism. That's results. But I'm going to be straight with you -- results cost money. And right now, I need to fund my team's operations through the entire month of April. We need to raise $92,430 to cover fieldwork, equipment, legal defense, and payroll for the journalists who made every one of those investigations possible. That's the real number. That's what it takes. $35 covers a background check on the voter identities being stolen in Los Angeles. $50 funds a day of undercover equipment. $100 covers a full day of field operations. $250 goes directly toward the legal defense fund protecting our journalists from the people who want us silenced. "" Bo Loudon, a supporter and political commentator, put it simply: "God bless James for uncovering this. These fraudsters NEED to be arrested." He's right. But arrests don't happen without evidence. Evidence doesn't happen without investigators. And investigators don't happen without you. We have four active investigations in the pipeline right now. Jonathan Choe, the journalist who broke the Los Angeles footage, is expanding into San Francisco's Tenderloin district. We're hiring new undercover operatives. The Citizen Justice League -- our network of trained citizen journalists -- is growing every week. But none of it moves forward without funding. Can I count on you for April? "" In Truth, James O'Keefe Founder and CEO O'Keefe Media Group P.S. 2.5 million people watched our footage. The White House responded. The DOJ promised action. The LA District Attorney opened a file. And a detective showed up at my door. All because of what your donations made possible. Now I need $92,430 to fund April. Your $35, $50, or $100 today is the reason we can keep going when they try to make us stop. "" Pop Acta Media | American Action News PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

**Good morning!** On this day in In 1921, KDKA in Pittsburgh broadcast [the first live sporting event on radio](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uhDAQBOCnMZ2RbWx-Chdp6C5tSrSHl2MTg5G9OsTbR5fkDdLOJ81I83GbbkCR9sc7bOh5SdOOZ4nIjHmCzLhRKUeEsoZ07l-wQYQCk1GmlcpKracAFK_JVsEviAabCr3uOjcoY5q2Wn2PzjjTQxeGtmss6K6zi0O93N196NtQkX-1Kau1Vlo1robBLT3Odp4dBq0HYRUG4pTrz0Q7r7jEKz0x13PaquhX5qOI5k2YUZjxPM96pcIpXy8XZuSVigxwSdrlnwgzwpEpSq2FGX9DmmU5UuYiMwRK8p4ThBkKlyr7cJwUI5W2E1Y9NqD4M87_euzpzXcAAAD__7vpgcg), as sports editor Florent Gibson called a Johnny Ray-Johnny Dundee boxing match. Just four months later, Harold Arlin announced the first major league baseball game on air.

Thanks to our many readers who responded to Friday's _Poll_ asking for your favorite 1960s sitcom, and plenty of you wrote in with favorites that didn't make our original list. So now we're presenting what any good '60s sitcom would do: a rerun. Today's _Poll_ offers a reader's edition, featuring the shows _you_ submitted. Same question, brand new options, all picked by you, our classic-TV aficionados. The conflict in Iran is dramatically affecting global oil markets, with crude oil briefly topping $100 a barrel this week. What does that mean for gas prices? On today's episode of _The Flyover Podcast_ , our team breaks down how rising oil prices are forecast to push gas and diesel prices even higher, with some Americans already paying nearly $6 a gallon. What does this mean for you and your family? ## Artemis II Astronauts Return Safely to Earth
NASA's Artemis II crew splashed down in the Pacific Ocean off San Diego at 8:07 p.m. ET on Friday, completing a historic 10-day, 695,081-mile journey around the moon, the first crewed lunar mission since Apollo 17 in 1972. [Here's a video of the return](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0DFvgzAQBeBfY7Yg32EgGTxUquiUdoo6Whf7CG4MjrATi39f0b_Q5Q3vSd_wvs_mTD745fZJM-s8RrNwSYFz5tXQmnn2KT0CpcnFstxppkCJDErsDlIdAIwjHzajKqdHZuSmYg19354kYtNVk772IyrasyNGOJKVPXXKMiC2wG3l9a5JBQASZNPWdGrHI1tlbcsO4CSUZOdzXOuf6Jc88Ri2-OK1tnGugp5yfiTRvAkcBA6llHqLz_y88r7vDWU7iWZ4iea9g0F-fF0udrhXq3aP4kPwqeuFkreZfPgj879-eGn8DQAA__9XJHEO). Commander Reid Wiseman, pilot Victor Glover, mission specialist Christina Koch, and Canadian mission specialist Jeremy Hansen returned safely [after setting a record](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1uhDAQBOCnMR3IXjA_hYs0dJc2JVrwcmxiMLJ9Z93bR0SR8gCpR5rRNx-36Ybs-Li_404mrX46KEdHKVGYMCTaOcbTYdysz8cX7ugw4gQS2lI2pVKTRXavqSmsWYmA6oKM6jo9SIC6LTaDFnQt5ayGftXazjjPsqYOW019v_ZLweZqk41SSipZ6woHvfa0NMuiySo1iEaS5eRD9en5SBut7uWfFKrF74UzW0pnFPWbgFHAmHOuljleiisXMDp-Uvk4LSaKAsZfVMlc_sHKQOkRDgFjEYw9MzvHse1EI-87svtZSv-652ngOwAA__8Bh3t6) for the farthest distance humans have ever traveled from Earth, 252,760 miles. Reentry posed significant risks because Orion's heat shield [had known design flaws](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLHOozAQhJ8GOiN7AQOFi2vocu2VaGMvYe-MjWwnKG9_Ivql_wH-alcazWi--XNbbsiew-M37mTKGpdAZ_ZUCqUFU6Gdcz485s3FM_zDHT1mXECCFrITSi0O2b-XrnZmJQJqazJqGPpJArS63owaV30fQE1DP7X9SKMerNUgiayzBFSzudJkp5SSSrZ9g1O_jmQ7a3tySk1VJ8lxian5GzmUjVb_ji9KjY177c1WypGr9lcFcwXzeZ5NuNuL4tIrmLNlCpau78DP9fwicffxUcEcMKP4AhXM4htWFN5JYC4pBnyWLD625-GwUBbJBgSt-0nVybjjZO8566Hq5GNH9p9u5UeDvgz8DwAA__9NR4zM), prompting NASA to plan a modified, steeper descent path to reduce exposure to extreme temperatures. Mission Control reported the landing as a perfect bullseye. Navy divers assisted the crew onto an inflatable raft before helicopters transported them to the USS John P. Murtha for medical evaluations ahead of their return to NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston.
## Trump Warns Iran to Open Strait
President Trump accused Iran of [doing a "very poor job"](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLFutDAQhJ8GOiN7wQYKF39Dd3-bEq1gOTaxMbJ951yePuJeIeVopBl938dtviE7Pu7_0ZPNW5gPKslRzhRnjJk8p3Q6TPsayvGFHh0mnEGCEbITSs0rsnvNXb3ajQiorcmqvtejBGhNvdthQ0MIctCgYSC1amNI98YsI-oRdc32WpOdUkoq2eoGR70NtHTLomlVaqw6SSvnEJvPwEfeaXOv8KTYLMHXzu45n6lq_1UwVTCVUpotfF8UV1_B5PhJ4soVTDk-_Ck44iEKRrEQJto4kkg5Imexh-gfP4JTRHJvQCnA1NGuZ2HnOJm-6uTdI7v3e_6TsqeF3wAAAP___eiBLg) allowing oil through the Strait of Hormuz and warned Tehran to stop charging fees to transiting ships, threatening renewed military action if the ceasefire terms aren't honored. The strait, which normally carries about 20% of the world's oil supply, [remains effectively closed](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L1u7CAUBOCnwR0WYIwvBcVt3G3alNYJHK_J8mMBWbR5-sh5hTTTjPSNNO-37QY--HR_g4im7XlL2GvA1rBsUBpGX-sZoB4u9_SACAEqbIIJRZmknG8OfHhtcnBmRxQ4DWj4ssyaCTGp4TASd1AWrVZaWnBsWmY9KSc443qZFRu8uTQmOeeMs2keQc_7P7TS2hkd55pIhs63XMbP7FM7cA-v_MQy2hyHYI7Wzkqm_0SsRKy999GmD3uVRKwXTcTK5BWaiNUXSLRDodkH2iA9sNDaCvhGj1zi1_d4tBiGYtzZfQi-qoVIdo_gw-9e-9NJTyN-AgAA___BIXpS) despite the two-week ceasefire announced Tuesday. Only a handful of vessels have passed through, far below the pre-war average of over 100 per day. Intelligence officials confirmed Iran's Revolutionary Guard Corps is controlling access and demanding fees from ships. Reports suggest Tehran plans to charge the cryptocurrency equivalent of $1 per barrel of oil on board. Vice President JD Vance [departed Friday for negotiations](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDtuwzAQRE8jdRT40ccqWKRR57QphTW5tDamRIFcW3BOHyhXSD2Ywbz3dZ2vQJG2-yesaDmkecOjRGTGPENmXKmUPUJZfDq2B6wQocCspe6FbIVSsweK77mtvQ2IGk2NVg1DN0qtTV8vNpjuIsHcUDscZT8Yh0EbA4Mc1XjxfU32XJOtUkoqaboGxi5c0LXOdeiVGqtWoidOuflOtPGCIb7TC3Pj0lpHuzDvpTIflZ4qPR3H0bhbOSnOvNJTpBeK5-6BsVR6ogybOCALzs91F4UzEIsUxJLy-vwRVDJgFA6hYKCMgiE-zmKdrd8PipFKP1StvK9A8e8D_0vcy-rfAAAA__-tEYSs) in Islamabad, Pakistan, warning Iran not to negotiate in bad faith. He is joined by envoy Steve Witkoff and Trump advisor Jared Kushner.
## Ranking: World's Largest Air Forces
A new ranking of global air power shows that the U.S. [dominates in the sky](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAQRp8GOiN7MH-Fi9vQ7W1TogkeL5MdMLKdRfv20eYV0n_6ztH5uC03ZOHj_h93ciXE5aArC5VCacFUaOecT8G8-XgdD9xRMOMCGnqlrTJm8cjyWmztXSACamtyZhi6SQO0fb25T2MJw9jqFUJvx7EHP4046SH4AG0PNbv3m7bGGG102zU4dWGk1a5rR96YqbKaPJeYmq_IR9koyCs-KTVr3GtxWylnrtp_FcwVzNd1NU_O3ygrnlxQOJf3sII54fEgr8pG6opJfFaC6U65KOSkQkwrZcWHeutUMNfJ-fNiEc79UFl935Hll1n-FOrp4CcAAP__V79-Ag), with 13,032 military aircraft, more than the next three countries combined. Russia ranks second at 4,237, with China third at 3,529. America's 1,791 fighters alone surpass many nations' entire fleets, according to [data from GlobalFirepower](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFugzAQgOGngS3INobA4KFLtnTtiC72Ga49MLJPsfL2VdtH6P7rG_6P-3IHYjrWd9jRSUzLgbUwimBeIAvuVMrJULaQ6vEFOzAUWIwy40XZi9ZLAOLXYtvgIqLBvkWnr9dhVsb0Y7u52Q_RqABhnFCP2is7gZ9mE5UfH9PsW3I_mrJaa6VVP3QwD3FCb70fMGg9N1ZhIEm5-0x0yIaRX-mJufNpb9ltImdp-rfG3Bpzq7V2K6cHcKSMZ6p_XWNuQNlniHKRJMDduZ1tduGsxExlvDZWrTsQ_6ryrxVPZ74DAAD__6t6dHc). Six of the top eight air forces are based in Asia, underscoring the region's growing military focus as countries like India, Japan, and South Korea rapidly modernize their aerial capabilities. The ranking also reveals that smaller air forces, such as those of France, Israel, and Taiwan, maintain strong combat readiness through concentrated investment in fighter aircraft. [See the full chart here](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1urDAQRp8GOiN7MH-Fi9vQ7W1TogkeL5MdMLKdRfv20eYV0n_6ztH5uC03ZOHj_h93ciXE5aArC5VCacFUaOecT8G8-XgdD9xRMOMCGnqlrTJm8cjyWmztXSACamtyZhi6SQO0fb25T2MJw9jqFUJvx7EHP4046SH4AG0PNbv3m7bGGG102zU4dWGk1a5rR96YqbKaPJeYmq_IR9koyCs-KTVr3GtxWylnrtp_FcwVzNd1NU_O3ygrnlxQOJf3sII54fEgr8pG6opJfFaC6U65KOSkQkwrZcWHeutUMNfJ-fNiEc79UFl935Hll1n-FOrp4CcAAP__V79-Ag).

name: Senator Johnson
subject: This isn't politics anymore. This is a permanent takeover.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ron Johnson for Senate Inc
body:
The Left is on a mission to take back the Senate, and there’s no coming back from what they have planned.

| | | | [](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckLuO2zAQRb-G6mTwKdkFiwUEpdp0QUqCIsfWbPgCScXI3wfaOPmAtGcu7uDc7-_m3WLA9PhqI-ja76Y203ds2FIvOWBH12z6FXOFvhtO-TRSOTJmSs2tgOuGDV575y1zA2g2z-pGhaJs2DW7qyt1k5J0U0pRpW5youAE2_iktkkMqM9CKhljVFAuLuoqb2remPNMyk1QIqkNUPulQjm2gM6mbtuPe64OLi7HIei999KIeCN8JXxt4I4KlyemCv5MEL7WnMaPvKeWE-Grhzg-se0BWx8hWgzjR7FErEePpuWjOiBiORGfThTB4xGJWMpn-EW7I2L58vZt_Cvw4s7GYvGRiFj-XOjVeIjt38uhal-eGAK2aSaSPs7ST5X-v_P_1Px3AAAA__8rApaw)
---

Friend,
The Left is on a mission to take back the Senate, and there’s no coming back from what they have planned.
It's no secret –
* _**STEP ONE: Eliminate the filibuster.**_ Simple majority rule replaces the 60-vote threshold that forces the Senate to actually build consensus.
* _**STEP TWO: Pack the Supreme Court.**_ Add four, five, six new justices — all chosen by a Democrat president, confirmed by a simple majority, and the constitutional guardrails we've spent decades rebuilding dismantled overnight.
* _**STEP THREE: Make D.C. a state.**_ Two new Democrat senators, automatically, permanently. They lock in their majority for a generation.

This isn’t hyperbole. It’s the agenda they’ve said OUT LOUD.
**_[Knowing all this, I have to ask you an important question: Will you donate today to STOP the Left’s permanent power grab?](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckLuO2zAQRb-G6mTwKdkFiwUEpdp0QUqCIsfWbPgCScXI3wfaOPmAtGcu7uDc7-_m3WLA9PhqI-ja76Y203ds2FIvOWBH12z6FXOFvhtO-TRSOTJmSs2tgOuGDV575y1zA2g2z-pGhaJs2DW7qyt1k5J0U0pRpW5youAE2_iktkkMqM9CKhljVFAuLuoqb2remPNMyk1QIqkNUPulQjm2gM6mbtuPe64OLi7HIei999KIeCN8JXxt4I4KlyemCv5MEL7WnMaPvKeWE-Grhzg-se0BWx8hWgzjR7FErEePpuWjOiBiORGfThTB4xGJWMpn-EW7I2L58vZt_Cvw4s7GYvGRiFj-XOjVeIjt38uhal-eGAK2aSaSPs7ST5X-v_P_1Px3AAAA__8rApaw)_**
The only thing standing between their radical wishlist and reality is Republican control of the Senate.
I've heard from a lot of voters lately who are frustrated. Angry, even.
Some are disenchanted. Some feel like nothing ever really changes — that we win elections and then watch the Establishment fumble it.
_**I understand that frustration. I share it.**_
I've spent my career in the Senate fighting a bureaucracy that fights back, a media that never gives us a fair shot, and a Democrat Party that plays for keeps in a way our side too often doesn't.
But here's what I know: Sitting it out doesn't punish the politicians you're angry at.
It hands power to the people who will destroy our country as we know it.
**[Are you willing to pitch in $5, $10, or $15 if it means stopping the Left’s permanent power grab? ](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckLuO2zAQRb-G6mTwKdkFiwUEpdp0QUqCIsfWbPgCScXI3wfaOPmAtGcu7uDc7-_m3WLA9PhqI-ja76Y203ds2FIvOWBH12z6FXOFvhtO-TRSOTJmSs2tgOuGDV575y1zA2g2z-pGhaJs2DW7qyt1k5J0U0pRpW5youAE2_iktkkMqM9CKhljVFAuLuoqb2remPNMyk1QIqkNUPulQjm2gM6mbtuPe64OLi7HIei999KIeCN8JXxt4I4KlyemCv5MEL7WnMaPvKeWE-Grhzg-se0BWx8hWgzjR7FErEePpuWjOiBiORGfThTB4xGJWMpn-EW7I2L58vZt_Cvw4s7GYvGRiFj-XOjVeIjt38uhal-eGAK2aSaSPs7ST5X-v_P_1Px3AAAA__8rApaw)**
I've been through brutal campaigns. I've had union bosses, liberal billionaires, and out-of-state activists pour tens of millions of dollars into defeating me.
**They failed because grassroots conservatives like you showed up.**
That's all I'm asking. Make a contribution. Get off the sidelines. Don't let the Left win by default.

[PITCH IN $5](https://email.alert.republicantaskforce.com/c/eJyckb2O3CAUhZ8Gd7b4tYeCYiXLqTZdlBJhYMd3w58AZ5S3jzyZ5AHSfvfoHn0639_1u4EA6f7VRK9q_9C16X5Ag5Z6yQE62GbSr5ir74emmM4j5iMhutTcirddk8EpZ50hdvCKLIuQmAlMhkPdpKQL48RJh7HDzjArxEJ2JoWQkogB1PUQc0IIZpiySdy4FMtOrCOc7wwjjk3wtU_Vl3MPYE3qpv34yNX6yeY4BHX0Xhpib4huiG7N27P66QGpenclEN1qTuNnPlLLCdHN-Tg-oB0BWh99NBDGz2IQ284edctntR6x9UJ0vlD0Ds6I2Fqe4RftFrH1y9u38a_Ai1sTi4F7Qmz9c8E37Xxs_yoRnU3MZ-qIrUQMVbnygBCgzQvi-H5VPMX6_47xU9HfAQAA__9ZYJrm)

clean_body: The Left is on a mission to take back the Senate, and there’s no coming back from what they have planned. | | | | Friend, The Left is on a mission to take back the Senate, and there’s no coming back from what they have planned. It's no secret – STEP ONE: Eliminate the filibuster. Simple majority rule replaces the 60-vote threshold that forces the Senate to actually build consensus. STEP TWO: Pack the Supreme Court. Add four, five, six new justices — all chosen by a Democrat president, confirmed by a simple majority, and the constitutional guardrails we've spent decades rebuilding dismantled overnight. STEP THREE: Make D.C. a state. Two new Democrat senators, automatically, permanently. They lock in their majority for a generation. This isn’t hyperbole. It’s the agenda they’ve said OUT LOUD. The only thing standing between their radical wishlist and reality is Republican control of the Senate. I've heard from a lot of voters lately who are frustrated. Angry, even. Some are disenchanted. Some feel like nothing ever really changes — that we win elections and then watch the Establishment fumble it. I understand that frustration. I share it. I've spent my career in the Senate fighting a bureaucracy that fights back, a media that never gives us a fair shot, and a Democrat Party that plays for keeps in a way our side too often doesn't. But here's what I know: Sitting it out doesn't punish the politicians you're angry at. It hands power to the people who will destroy our country as we know it. I've been through brutal campaigns. I've had union bosses, liberal billionaires, and out-of-state activists pour tens of millions of dollars into defeating me. They failed because grassroots conservatives like you showed up. That's all I'm asking. Make a contribution. Get off the sidelines. Don't let the Left win by default. Because if they take the Senate back – if they eliminate the filibuster, pack the court, add two permanent Democrat senators from D.C. – that's the ballgame. This is the moment for action. Thank you for stepping up with whatever you can, \- Ron Johnson** | | | PO Box 1159 Oshkosh, WI 54903 | | Paid for by Ron Johnson for Senate Inc. | | Pop Acta Media | Republican Task Force PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 - | _

| | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw
---
Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18]

clean_body: Companies are already delaying their IPOs. Why?| | | Don't want to receive our publications? | | | Lifе and death Son of Mykhailo Sulyma, Ivan came from a petty noble (szlachta) family. He was born in Rohoshchi (next to Chernihiv). He served as an estate overseer for Stanisław Żółkiewski and later the family of Daniłowicze who inherited his lands; for that service in 1620 he was awarded three villages: Sulimówka, Kuczakiw and Lebedyn. Аll the villages todаy belong to the Boryspil Raion, Kyiv Oblast. His sons included Stepan (died 1659), a captain of Boryspil company, and Fedir (died 1691), a colonel of Pereiaslav regiment. He became popular among the unregistered Cossacks, leading them on campaigns to plunder Crimea and other Ottoman vassal territories. For organizing a revolt on an Ottoman slave galley and freeing Christian slaves[1] he received a medal from Pope Paul V himself. Eventually, Sulyma reached the rank of the hetman, which he held from 1628 to 1629 and 1630 to 1635. In 1635, after returning from an expedition to Black Sea against the Ottomans, he decided to rebel against the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth, which at that time controlled most of the Cossack territories, and whose nobility was trying to turn militant Cossacks into serfs.[citation needed] Ivan Sulyma took part in numerous campaigns of Sagaidachny against Tatars and Turks. In particular, it was the famous capture of Kafa (modern Theodosia), the main center of the slave trade on the Black Sea, Trapezont, Izmail, and also two attacks on Tsaregrad. On the night of 3 to 4 August 1635 he took the newly constructed Kodak fortress by surprise, burning it and executing its crew of about 200 people under Jean Marion. Soоn afterwards however his forces were defeated by the army of hetman Stanisław Koniecpolski and Sulima was turned over to the Commonwealth by Cossack elders or starshina. Together with several other leaders of his rebellion, Hetman Sulyma was executed in Warsaw on 12 December 1635. At first, the Polish King Władysław IV Waza, known for his friendly attitude towards the Cossacks, was hesitant to execute Sulyma, especially since he was a person upon whom the Pope himself bestowed his medal. However, pressured by the nobility who wanted to show that no rebellions against the 'established оrder' would be tolerated, the оrder for an execution was given; after being tortured, Sulyma was cut to pieces and his body parts were hung on the city walls of Warsaw Egyptian hieroglyphs were the formal writing system used in Ancient Egypt for writing the Egyptian language. Hieroglyphs combined logographic, syllabic and alphabetic elements, with some 1,000 distinct characters.[3][4] Cursive hieroglyphs were used for religious literature on papyrus and wood. The later hieratic and demotic Egyptian scripts were derived from hieroglyphic writing, as was the Proto-Sinaitic script that later evolved into the Phoenician alphabet.[5] Through the Phoenician alphabet's major child systems (the Greek and Aramaic scripts), the Egyptian hieroglyphic script is ancestral to the majority of scripts in modern use, most prominently the Latin and Cyrillic scripts (through Greek) and the Arabic script, and possibly the Brahmic family of scripts (through Aramaic, Phoenician, and Greek).[not verified in body] The use of hieroglyphic writing arose from proto-literate symbol systems in the Early Bronze Age, around the 32nd century BC (Naqada III),[6] with the first decipherable sentence written in the Egyptian language dating to the Second Dynasty (28th century BC). Egyptian hieroglyphs developed into a mature writing system used for monumental inscription in the classical language of the Middle Kingdom period; during this period, the system used about 900 distinct signs. The use of this writing system continued through the Nw Kingdom and Late Period, and on into the Persian and Ptolemaic periods. Late survivals of hieroglyphic use are found well into the Roman period, extending into the 4th century AD.[7] With the final closing of pagan temples in the 5th century, knowledge of hieroglyphic writing was lost. Although attempts were made, the script remained undeciphered throughout the Middle Ages and the early modern period. The decipherment of hieroglyphic writing was finally accomplished in the 1820s by Jean-François Champollion, with the help of the Rosetta Stone.[8] The number of words contained in al Ancient Egyptian (i.e. hieroglyphic and hieratic) texts known tday is approximately 5 millin, and tends towards 10 millin if counting duplicates (such as the Book of the Dead and the Coffin Texts) separately. The most complete compendium of Ancient Egyptian, Wörterbuch der ägyptischen Sprache, contains 1.5–1.7 millin words.[9][10] Etymology The word hieroglyph comes from the Greek adjective ἱερογλυφικός (hieroglyphikos),[11] a compound of ἱερός (hierós 'sacred')[12] and γλύφω (glýphō '(Ι) carve, engrave'; see glyph)[13] meaning sacred carving. The glyphs themselves, since the Ptolemaic period, were called τὰ ἱερογλυφικὰ [γράμματα] (tà hieroglyphikà [grámmata]) "the sacred engraved letters", the Greek counterpart to the Egyptian expression of mdw.w-nṯr "god's words".[14] Greek ἱερόγλυφος meant "a carver of hieroglyphs".[15] In English, hieroglyph as a noun is recorded from 1590, originally short for nominalized hieroglyphic (1580s, with a plural hieroglyphics), from adjectival use (hieroglyphic character).[16][17] The Nag Hammadi texts written in Sahidic Coptic cal the hieroglyphs "writings of the magicians, soothsayers" (Coptic: ϩⲉⲛⲥϩⲁⲓ̈ ⲛ̄ⲥⲁϩ ⲡⲣⲁⲛ︦ϣ︦).[18] | | | | | Companies are already delaying their IPOs. name: Houston Gaines
subject: Sine Die Updates from the Gold Dome
body:
Read up-to-date information from the Capitol
| | | | |
---
| Last week, we concluded the 2026 legislative Session, adjourning Sine Die and completing my final session under the Gold Dome. It has been an incredible honor to represent you--and I'm proud of what we've accomplished together as we now look ahead to taking that fight to Congress.
Please read below for a final recap and key updates from this year's Session.
---
| |
---
| LEGISLATIVE UPDATES
---
|
---
| Last Thursday evening, the House and Senate finished its work around 1 a.m. after more than 12 hours of voting on the House floor. On the final day, the House considered several dozen bills. As I completed my final Session under the Gold Dome, Gabi joined me on the House floor for our final Sine Die. It was a bittersweet moment as we completed one chapter and look towards the next.
The General Assembly's sole Constitutional responsibility each year is to pass a balanced budget. On the final day of the Session, we did just that as the House and Senate came to an agreement and approved the fiscal year 2027 state budget. It funds key priorities shared by both chambers. This budget makes investments in Georgia's workforce, retirees, and students--reinforcing our commitment to strong communities and long-term growth across the state. Below are just a few highlights:
* $70 million to expand literacy coaches into every elementary school statewide
* $11.5 million to fund an additional 900 NOW/COMP waivers to expand services to individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities
* $9.5 million for start up costs at the new University of Georgia School of Medicine to grow medical training capacity and address Georgia's healthcare workforce needs
* $100 million in additional funding for the Employee Retirement System of Georgia, which could support a 2.25% cost-of-living adjustment, pending board approval

For additional highlights, [click here](https://a89bm49ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001j1WVUDr8HY6fJNROdJoqKxbBHcAlxHr0x_TXKhXdCMxEfRKW0uH3JrdG5Cbkg6R8E9l7eTq1uWMehd3aQa318aCeURsvydJySoBNXm_LtR_DL0xFf1hYWHTNWKnVlUh1LFdRvlGN3xIQh3qs-Dt0BqL1bTT8w9fNU3f9kuP--aN24duZHCxHlDIsBPhegTkxd88ypeprXGdhEJxVLzbAj4UO8nxj5qhyv9q9gdDe34Y=&c=dwN_z8Qn8889ZyQQXhiq8wZ91ir58TlY5sq0leTp77PL6SQvc_g65A==&ch=8WXqkMgnqP153ZCwN_WHeFvpB5sPVl3jbw2zXP5u74GOj8mOy95tgw==).
This Session, we continued our commitment to returning money to taxpayers through multiple avenues, including immediate and long-term income tax cuts. HB 463 takes Georgia's income tax rate to 4.99% immediately and down to 3.99% over time and significantly increases the standard deduction. This puts more dollars directly back into families' pockets, strengthens Georgia's competitive business environment, and encourages continued economic growth and job creation across the state. This tax relief comes in addition to the fourth consecutive one-time income tax rebate (each individual filer will receive $250, joint filers will receive $500).
While we began the Session with hopes of more aggressive property tax relief, we did ultimately pass the Homeownership Opportunity and Market Equalization Act of 2026 (SB 33). The bill makes the statewide homestead exemption uniform by removing local opt-outs beginning in 2027, ensuring a maximum increase in assessed values at 3% or the rate of inflation. It also gives counties the option to adopt a Local Homestead Option Sales Tax to provide additional property tax relief. This legislation is designed to provide greater stability and predictability for homeowners while giving local communities a new tool to help lower property tax burdens. This legislation is in addition to Homeowners Grant Relief the legislature gave final approval to earlier this year that will provide approximately $500 in relief this year on homeowners' property tax bill.
One of my bills, House Bill 295, received final passage around midnight on the final day. This measure will hold local governments accountable if they fail to uphold existing ordinances and laws related to homelessness or illegal immigration. Property owners will now have a way to seek a refund on their property taxes in jurisdictions where the city or county fails to enforce laws related to illegal camping, panhandling, loitering, shoplifting, and other similar issues. The refund must be based on either expenses incurred (i.e. security cameras, fencing, etc.) or on diminished value of the property and is eligible up to the taxes paid. This law holds local governments accountable when they fail to enforce the law--giving property owners a path to recover costs when their communities are ignored.
Another of my bills, House Bill 1379, received final passage, as well. It represents a continued effort by the General Assembly to strengthen transparency and accountability. The legislation requires public disclosure throughout our education system of any foreign funding from countries of concern. We've seen in recent years an alarming rise in countries providing funding to both K-12 and higher education institutions with the goal of influencing curriculum. The public should know about this. Read more about this bill [here](https://a89bm49ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001j1WVUDr8HY6fJNROdJoqKxbBHcAlxHr0x_TXKhXdCMxEfRKW0uH3JrdG5Cbkg6R8-YEfBxHAs6rAalQmiWF0LqNTUwberKpb_5_VcDklFYB5KDSKiiNG2SY8xt8_BT0bqgq9ppWgQUmyZbuIUCoV3cc8R0Y-igQqloGlKzbBh4iG3DPdFCU051H4xxpZ8NjJ3uwpiQ1atngrUTqvErzLovGitHwA8YS89gtdikeKl2uwS_nZznfgniNyHVI1qjR0g3qK4qB9ODs=&c=dwN_z8Qn8889ZyQQXhiq8wZ91ir58TlY5sq0leTp77PL6SQvc_g65A==&ch=8WXqkMgnqP153ZCwN_WHeFvpB5sPVl3jbw2zXP5u74GOj8mOy95tgw==).
One area where the General Assembly failed to reach agreement is around our elections. There are currently QR codes on each ballot, but come July 1, these QR codes must be removed per Georgia law. However, the Secretary of State has failed to do so as required. This issue must be resolved either by a special session of the legislature, through the courts, or another process. There are ongoing discussions to address this issue.
---
| |
---
| In addition to these headline measures, the General Assembly gave final approval to dozens of other bills, including:
* HB 1187: This bill, Trey's Law, would make nondisclosure agreements unenforceable if they prevent survivors from speaking about childhood sexual abuse. Inspired by Trey Carlock's story, the bill aims to prevent further harm to survivors by ensuring they can speak openly without being silenced by legal agreements.
* HB 651: This measure will shift oversight of school zone speed cameras to the Department of Transportation, set stricter rules for when citations can be issued, and require clear safety features like flashing lights. It also increases accountability by limiting fines and fees, enforcing transparency, and requiring voter approval every six years for communities to continue using these devices.
* HB 1193: The Georgia Early Literacy Act of 2026 aims to strengthen early reading education by expanding literacy funding, requiring evidence-based instruction, and providing literacy coaches and training for K-3 teachers. It also increases accountability through statewide standards and new leadership roles to ensure students develop strong reading skills from an early age.
* HB 1344: This bill is aimed at holding insurance companies accountable and helping consumers by increasing more than 40 fines for bad actors in the insurance market and making claims processing faster, fairer and more transparent. The legislation is part of a broader effort to modernize regulations and help stabilize insurance costs while protecting consumers across the state.

All of these measures will now head to Governor Kemp's desk for his approval before becoming law. The Governor has 40 days from the end of the legislative Session to sign or veto legislation.
---
| |
---
| IN THE NEWS
---
| | [](https://a89bm49ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001j1WVUDr8HY6fJNROdJoqKxbBHcAlxHr0x_TXKhXdCMxEfRKW0uH3JrdG5Cbkg6R8yya9AGW7Qm15Ao9pQnxubAqqtYdHFjX0WhflRYCot2UagBZrmwlNLtc04ouRxF3_6baEc5EOcETma3gC7H1J7QUi9k4JWNlA8F4B76nZRB0zlGca790yfuCBnLchcu58VnZvoyhWH92OQy4lmM2J2SIgH107Z6W3tLz3a0yGRG9dETbQjzY7_H77tQWZQtyuADftYzuh6yyJwafRt0RFbxgtEhfnOYSN&c=dwN_z8Qn8889ZyQQXhiq8wZ91ir58TlY5sq0leTp77PL6SQvc_g65A==&ch=8WXqkMgnqP153ZCwN_WHeFvpB5sPVl3jbw2zXP5u74GOj8mOy95tgw==)
---
[Scenic Southern cities tarnished by homeless crime scourge must hold 'feet to the fire,' GOP lawmaker warns](https://a89bm49ab.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001j1WVUDr8HY6fJNROdJoqKxbBHcAlxHr0x_TXKhXdCMxEfRKW0uH3JrdG5Cbkg6R8yya9AGW7Qm15Ao9pQnxubAqqtYdHFjX0WhflRYCot2UagBZrmwlNLtc04ouRxF3_6baEc5EOcETma3gC7H1J7QUi9k4JWNlA8F4B76nZRB0zlGca790yfuCBnLchcu58VnZvoyhWH92OQy4lmM2J2SIgH107Z6W3tLz3a0yGRG9dETbQjzY7_H77tQWZQtyuADftYzuh6yyJwafRt0RFb0NfwD1AoCPX&c=dwN_z8Qn8889ZyQQXhiq8wZ91ir58TlY5sq0leTp77PL6SQvc_g65A==&ch=8WXqkMgnqP153ZCwN_WHeFvpB5sPVl3jbw2zXP5u74GOj8mOy95tgw==)
"State Rep. Houston Gaines, who is running for Congress, is backing legislation aimed at forcing cities like Savannah, Athens and Atlanta to more aggressively enforce laws on illegal camping, loitering and panhandling."
|
---
EVENTS AND VISITORS
---
| On Sine Die, I was especially grateful to have my father and mother visit to celebrate the close of my time under the Gold Dome.
Last week, I also had the pleasure of attending the St. Mary's Celebration of Courageous Leaders Gala with my family, supporting a great cause. I kicked off Easter weekend at an egg hunt with my nephews and spent Easter morning at Ashford Methodist Church in Watkinsville.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| PAGE PROGRAM
---
| I enjoyed welcoming three pages at the Capitol recently - William Ryan, Lincoln Vonk, and Milania Bernardi. The page program gives students the opportunity to spend a day with us in the House, learning firsthand about the work we do under the Gold Dome. We're grateful to these outstanding students for taking the time to visit the Capitol and for their interest in being involved and engaged in the legislative process.
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| |
---
| WHAT'S NEXT?
---
| While I'll remain a State Representative through the end of 2026, last week marked my eighth and final legislative Session under the Gold Dome. I'm deeply grateful for the opportunity you've given me to serve--and I look forward to continuing our work together in the years ahead.
As always, I remain available to assist with any questions, comments, or concerns you may have--please don't hesitate to reach out. It has been a true honor to serve as your State Representative.

clean_body: Read up-to-date information from the Capitol | | | | | | Last week, we concluded the 2026 legislative Session, adjourning Sine Die and completing my final session under the Gold Dome. It has been an incredible honor to represent you--and I'm proud of what we've accomplished together as we now look ahead to taking that fight to Congress. Please read below for a final recap and key updates from this year's Session. | | | LEGISLATIVE UPDATES | | Last Thursday evening, the House and Senate finished its work around 1 a.m. after more than 12 hours of voting on the House floor. On the final day, the House considered several dozen bills. As I completed my final Session under the Gold Dome, Gabi joined me on the House floor for our final Sine Die. It was a bittersweet moment as we completed one chapter and look towards the next. The General Assembly's sole Constitutional responsibility each year is to pass a balanced budget. On the final day of the Session, we did just that as the House and Senate came to an agreement and approved the fiscal year 2027 state budget. It funds key priorities shared by both chambers. This budget makes investments in Georgia's workforce, retirees, and students--reinforcing our commitment to strong communities and long-term growth across the state. Below are just a few highlights: $70 million to expand literacy coaches into every elementary school statewide $11.5 million to fund an additional 900 NOW/COMP waivers to expand services to individuals with intellectual and developmental disabilities $9.5 million for start up costs at the new University of Georgia School of Medicine to grow medical training capacity and address Georgia's healthcare workforce needs $100 million in additional funding for the Employee Retirement System of Georgia, which could support a 2.25% cost-of-living adjustment, pending board approval For additional highlights, ==. This Session, we continued our commitment to returning money to taxpayers through multiple avenues, including immediate and long-term income tax cuts. HB 463 takes Georgia's income tax rate to 4.99% immediately and down to 3.99% over time and significantly increases the standard deduction. This puts more dollars directly back into families' pockets, strengthens Georgia's competitive business environment, and encourages continued economic growth and job creation across the state. This tax relief comes in addition to the fourth consecutive one-time income tax rebate (each individual filer will receive $250, joint filers will receive $500). While we began the Session with hopes of more aggressive property tax relief, we did ultimately pass the Homeownership Opportunity and Market Equalization Act of 2026 (SB 33). The bill makes the statewide homestead exemption uniform by removing local opt-outs beginning in 2027, ensuring a maximum increase in assessed values at 3% or the rate of inflation. It also gives counties the option to adopt a Local Homestead Option Sales Tax to provide additional property tax relief. This legislation is designed to provide greater stability and predictability for homeowners while giving local communities a new tool to help lower property tax burdens. This legislation is in addition to Homeowners Grant Relief the legislature gave final approval to earlier this year that will provide approximately $500 in relief this year on homeowners' property tax bill. One of my bills, House Bill 295, received final passage around midnight on the final day. This measure will hold local governments accountable if they fail to uphold existing ordinances and laws related to homelessness or illegal immigration. Property owners will now have a way to seek a refund on their property taxes in jurisdictions where the city or county fails to enforce laws related to illegal camping, panhandling, loitering, shoplifting, and other similar issues. The refund must be based on either expenses incurred (i.e. security cameras, fencing, etc.) or on diminished value of the property and is eligible up to the taxes paid. This law holds local governments accountable when they fail to enforce the law--giving property owners a path to recover costs when their communities are ignored. Another of my bills, House Bill 1379, received final passage, as well. It represents a continued effort by the General Assembly to strengthen transparency and accountability. The legislation requires public disclosure throughout our education system of any foreign funding from countries of concern. We've seen in recent years an alarming rise in countries providing funding to both K-12 and higher education institutions with the goal of influencing curriculum. The public should know about this. Read more about this bill ==. One area where the General Assembly failed to reach agreement is around our elections. There are currently QR codes on each ballot, but come July 1, these QR codes must be removed per Georgia law. However, the Secretary of State has failed to do so as required. This issue must be resolved either by a special session of the legislature, through the courts, or another process. There are ongoing discussions to address this issue. | | | In addition to these headline measures, the General Assembly gave final approval to dozens of other bills, including: HB 1187: This bill, Trey's Law, would make nondisclosure agreements unenforceable if they prevent survivors from speaking about childhood sexual abuse. Inspired by Trey Carlock's story, the bill aims to prevent further harm to survivors by ensuring they can speak openly without being silenced by legal agreements. HB 651: This measure will shift oversight of school zone speed cameras to the Department of Transportation, set stricter rules for when citations can be issued, and require clear safety features like flashing lights. It also increases accountability by limiting fines and fees, enforcing transparency, and requiring voter approval every six years for communities to continue using these devices. HB 1193: The Georgia Early Literacy Act of 2026 aims to strengthen early reading education by expanding literacy funding, requiring evidence-based instruction, and providing literacy coaches and training for K-3 teachers. It also increases accountability through statewide standards and new leadership roles to ensure students develop strong reading skills from an early age. HB 1344: This bill is aimed at holding insurance companies accountable and helping consumers by increasing more than 40 fines for bad actors in the insurance market and making claims processing faster, fairer and more transparent. The legislation is part of a broader effort to modernize regulations and help stabilize insurance costs while protecting consumers across the state. All of these measures will now head to Governor Kemp's desk for his approval before becoming law. The Governor has 40 days from the end of the legislative Session to sign or veto legislation. | | | IN THE NEWS | | == == "State Rep. Houston Gaines, who is running for Congress, is backing legislation aimed at forcing cities like Savannah, Athens and Atlanta to more aggressively enforce laws on illegal camping, loitering and panhandling." | EVENTS AND VISITORS | On Sine Die, I was especially grateful to have my father and mother visit to celebrate the close of my time under the Gold Dome. Last week, I also had the pleasure of attending the St. Mary's Celebration of Courageous Leaders Gala with my family, supporting a great cause. I kicked off Easter weekend at an egg hunt with my nephews and spent Easter morning at Ashford Methodist Church in Watkinsville. | | | | | | | | | PAGE PROGRAM | I enjoyed welcoming three pages at the Capitol recently - William Ryan, Lincoln Vonk, and Milania Bernardi. The page program gives students the opportunity to spend a day with us in the House, learning firsthand about the work we do under the Gold Dome. We're grateful to these outstanding students for taking the time to visit the Capitol and for their interest in being involved and engaged in the legislative process. | | | | | | | | | WHAT'S NEXT? | While I'll remain a State Representative through the end of 2026, last week marked my eighth and final legislative Session under the Gold Dome. I'm deeply grateful for the opportunity you've given me to serve--and I look forward to continuing our work together in the years ahead. As always, I remain available to assist with any questions, comments, or concerns you may have--please don't hesitate to reach out. It has been a true honor to serve as your State Representative. | == == == | | == | OUR OFFICE 401-A Coverdell Legislative Office Building Atlanta, GA 30334 404.656.5025 Houston.Gaines@house.ga.gov | | | | | | Committee to Elect Houston Gaines | P.O. Box 1203 | Athens, GA 30603 US |

name: The Texas Flyover
subject: Death Sentence Overturned, Severe Weather Warning, and a Top-Ranked Pizza
body:
The state's Court of Criminal Appeals has overturned the [death sentence](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQgOGnwRvWgSF2BoYu3tK1o3WFc4xETASXOH77ypGqPkD3X3f33ddlumBMcb1-4o0cv-Y8rbTVRMxUpkDIS6WVafWUn1T4UVYKlSYN-iTBSKWmgDHtk2mC-4ZwVg051ff2DLaztlkcdF6htmZA6tB3vpsHi8YoUGciQ30T3TEMjFJKgdZDaywY8N4jUI8DkDBAIXIuLS_E9MI6p_24pvX51iS3MN-r6D6EHoUet21rl_yonNd7oVqPSOjxUAk9vkXylyT_TBJnpiLNIHfCcqRNceG-xZRiPfXCwPWGMb1X8v8e9XT6JwAA___dV37j) for Clarence Jordan, who has been on death row for 48 years, one of the nation's longest running death sentences.

| | |
---
| [](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL1u4zAQhJ-G6mQsKa4pFSzuYKjzuTkg6YQVubSIUD-Q6L-3DxynSJ92Pszgw7wduyPFFKfzPxrZ5nuYu4lvW-Kcee08Ux42njJPjucrr_myTuw37hSofQm6lLLzFNOj04W3PfhGFmylMdgAVojFYNn5Xml0SKYhHQI3qLlCyUr3howron2OgZZSSlCq3mkEDc45AjZUAwsN7GOe110eOPOdtpAeT5udm8ci2SHnZRPVH6FaodrEiV36eDKh2qrXTS1lVTqvodTssexrBSUpGSAEdOhAVK2jcaF4nrroRXUQ5u_PwByE2i-00vhiJ5TlSSF8g0v_av1_b0_Fav1yiynFbW-EhvNIMX1p5t-de7XqMwAA___aVIlA) | |
# Saturday, April 11, 2026
---|---
**Good Morning!** On this day in 1917, novelist and World War II prisoner of war [David Westheimer](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGO6yAQRb8Gd7YAg3EKite4y2u3tCaeSUDBEMFsnPz9KvsL21_dc3S-zusZYor59h928vy6ljXT0RIxU12RgEOjzJQ3Kk-q_F0zYaNVSz310vRKrQgxvVfTob9IPKmOvHLOnqQdre2C1wjj9WINKtTOzsZNBmZtJq2sO8Fou-g_Z9IopZTUeh6MlUZu2waSHMyShJGEkUsdOBDTC9o1vT82w1b2LvnA_Ghi_Cf0IvRyHMfALUDJKWYaSr0JvQTIeCnlLvRCmWuk9llS40Bxp9ojPCP2d3gkyF31-DhiSrFNThh52yGmXxT_LdDT658AAAD__1hMe6Q) was born in Houston. A B-24 navigator, he was shot down over Italy in 1942. After the war, he became a writer whose famous work is the 1964 novel _Von Ryan 's Express_, later made into a movie starring Frank Sinatra and Trevor Howard. The [best pizza in Texas](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD1u4zAUhE9Ddc_gnyypULGNumy7pfBMjiICtGSQb8M4p1842BuknQFm5ps_b-sbp5yO9998xyyf27keaDVDBGWNYNkrDsERcH6gyN9yIFasVtsraU_GrJFTfq6-i_NNx8l0mM0w9JPuXd93-zyY4RquCMYFOD_5CPb6Fm9sMY2A79L8CtPeGGO0tePF99rrEAJrDDxqKK8Rk5zlIjsEn1y3_HytuYTz3uV5F3lU5X4puyi7tNYubWN-mcouXCSFDGWXF5eySz4DZ2WXuDV6pK8vpvo4hVo6Kt1Q5b-YDvquIm5cIm1nIdlBFeE8Ij3BhXYUVGr7U9nFjgN5F8MG5yhsxpPfLNM0GUNw02iHCO08ujLHR0s5p3odlNfvd075m0N-9v7HbP8FAAD__6YBmaE) is a Detroit-style pie? Good thing we're not talking about barbecue! Read the story in today's _Et Cetera_ section. The conflict in Iran is dramatically affecting global oil markets, with crude oil briefly topping $100 a barrel this week. What does that mean for gas prices? On today's episode of _The Flyover Podcast_ , our team breaks down how rising oil prices are forecast to push gas and diesel prices even higher, with some Americans already paying nearly $6 a gallon. What does this mean for you and your family? ## Court Overturns 48-Year Death Sentence
The state's Court of Criminal Appeals has overturned the [death sentence](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJyk0LFuwyAQgOGnwRvWgSF2BoYu3tK1o3WFc4xETASXOH77ypGqPkD3X3f33ddlumBMcb1-4o0cv-Y8rbTVRMxUpkDIS6WVafWUn1T4UVYKlSYN-iTBSKWmgDHtk2mC-4ZwVg051ff2DLaztlkcdF6htmZA6tB3vpsHi8YoUGciQ30T3TEMjFJKgdZDaywY8N4jUI8DkDBAIXIuLS_E9MI6p_24pvX51iS3MN-r6D6EHoUet21rl_yonNd7oVqPSOjxUAk9vkXylyT_TBJnpiLNIHfCcqRNceG-xZRiPfXCwPWGMb1X8v8e9XT6JwAA___dV37j) for Clarence Jordan, who has been on death row for 48 years, one of the nation's longest running death sentences. Jordan, who has intellectual disabilities and schizophrenia, was sentenced to death for the 1977 murder of Houston grocer Joe L. Williams. Jordan's case was one of many delayed criminal appeals in Harris County that came to light in 2024, when new legal counsel was appointed for him. The state's highest criminal court agreed that the jury that sentenced Jordan, now 70, was not able to fully consider his [mental illness](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2O4zAMRk8jdwwkWv5JoWIbd7PtlgYjcWItZCmQmMnk9gsbmBNs_UDie-_Px_pBMcV8_007O_n-LGvmV0sswnUNTLI1zsLZc_niKs-aOTReUeMI2oIxa6CY3qvtgrvpcDUdOzNNw1UP_TB0m-sZrfWDRt3jiHQlxPFqR4PkPfYDdtEdz7Q1xhiNOF_soK323pPmiWbNymoOUUq9yMbC39Q-0_tYc_Fl75LbRB5N9b8ULgoXunnTH0Th8ihNFC7nDWzxvnET8DXuMVMCX55V4EerwY8obFRrbAfP8oaYdxIGn6ie1D-rxAZ_Sw2U4cYx38FO8GaqDc5iUMtL4WLmeRxxnhUuXXXh8YopxTZOyur7TjGd8-X_on85_BcAAP__Ce6YZQ) at the time of the trial, overturning his death sentence to life with a chance of parole.
## Education Board OKs Bible as Required Reading
The Texas State Board of Education gave [preliminary approval](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkbGOo0AMhp8GOkfDACEUFNek22uvRGbGBEvODBo72c3bn4jyBlvasj5_9v_va_5CFk63v3inyX7WPCf6ViEzKnMktE0pGaVA-UnFHiVRVJq982dwHTTNHJHlNXd1nBYXx6amqRmGfnR92_f1Nq2XMVxcXNo2dH5c29ieBxyJ-kBE_RBrng6Y65qmaZz3l1PXu86FENDRgBdHVecosuVyso2MflBXeR02p5DvtUyb2a5V-6fy18pfD_1OMWGynDgfM59u5a-SA0rlr28KUHwENM4Jlowlwo2fpLAXEr5zwvIC3PeSnyhgGQph5HQDYTXgFOTxLhdehCDk40kGigk-m0HtESmZghGGjYqCGq4rCKnmBLtg0rpMcf9mEdbzUHXudkeW9132uzSek_8fAAD__85Lpw0) to a new required reading list that includes biblical texts. The list of required reading material, which would go into effect in 2030, can undergo further revisions before pending [final approval](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OwyAQRk-DO6JhjP8Kim3cZZtotaWFYRIjEWPBJE5uv3KusPUnPb3v_Z6nsw0xrLdveyfDr2uaVtpLJGbKkyfLS6GVaXWUnpT5kVfyhSYEbCVoqdTkbYjvSVfezOAHVZFRXdcM0NRNUy1mvgLUCNq16FqH1HjQBIQzdlb5zlbBHDDQSikFiP1JN6DBOWeBOtsDCQ3kA6d84oWYXrZc4_uwObl0r6JZmLci6i-Bo8DRzu54cGwCx5-LwHEPmS6c8lvgOIc5kiyccqAiN5tZrrTLD1ZujzkGJ4tbUopFZrI-rDepatWrfmiHKhu_7SHGUNpOaLjdbYgfC_5fu6fBvwAAAP__5qmEcw) in June. The board heard hours of public testimony this week, leading up to the eventual 9-1 vote for approval, with critics saying the reading list is too long, emphasizes Christianity, and underrepresents Black and Hispanic authors. The initial required reading list recommended by the Texas Education Agency was pared down from roughly 300 to 200 titles, but still includes readings from the Bible.

Greg Abbott has activated [emergency resources](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHSoyAUhZ9GOxwgoLGg2MYu227p3MAxMouYgbsxefsd8wp_d4s753zf-XObbxRTzI_ftMHxe9nnjKMmMKPMAcRrRWZkj_2Fwv9KRqiYtdS9kEYoNQeK6TObNri7DKNq4dQw2FHai7Xt6rQk6j1075dRjlc72MsQ7kYvfdCjxdBGd4ZJo5RSUutrZ6w00ntPEgNdJRojESLvpeMVjDfVJX1Oms7vW5vcyvyszeVXo6dGT8dxdMv-NqfG-dDo6TwbPQVKiao4TikUUfFCgai8l03wWkAsYvYFVFFF3guv4tsmDuAvcmiLC88jphRrPzRGPjaK6cvAP1vu5fT_AAAA___Ip4YJ) ahead of potentially severe weather over the weekend and into next week. On this day in 1917, novelist and World War II prisoner of war was born in Houston. A B-24 navigator, he was shot down over Italy in 1942. After the war, he became a writer whose famous work is the 1964 novel Von Ryan 's Express, later made into a movie starring Frank Sinatra and Trevor Howard. The is a Detroit-style pie? Good thing we're not talking about barbecue! Read the story in today's Et Cetera section. The conflict in Iran is dramatically affecting global oil markets, with crude oil briefly topping $100 a barrel this week. What does that mean for gas prices? On today's episode of The Flyover Podcast , our team breaks down how rising oil prices are forecast to push gas and diesel prices even higher, with some Americans already paying nearly $6 a gallon. What does this mean for you and your family? . Say goodbye to backbreaking scrubbing and tangled cords. Busy day? Listen to our daily 15-minute podcast while commuting, cooking, or walking the dog--your Flyover fix, anywhere. | | Court Overturns 48-Year Death Sentence The state's Court of Criminal Appeals has overturned the for Clarence Jordan, who has been on death row for 48 years, one of the nation's longest running death sentences. Jordan, who has intellectual disabilities and schizophrenia, was sentenced to death for the 1977 murder of Houston grocer Joe L. Williams. Jordan's case was one of many delayed criminal appeals in Harris County that came to light in 2024, when new legal counsel was appointed for him. The state's highest criminal court agreed that the jury that sentenced Jordan, now 70, was not able to fully consider his at the time of the trial, overturning his death sentence to life with a chance of parole. Education Board OKs Bible as Required Reading The Texas State Board of Education gave to a new required reading list that includes biblical texts. The list of required reading material, which would go into effect in 2030, can undergo further revisions before pending in June. The board heard hours of public testimony this week, leading up to the eventual 9-1 vote for approval, with critics saying the reading list is too long, emphasizes Christianity, and underrepresents Black and Hispanic authors. The initial required reading list recommended by the Texas Education Agency was pared down from roughly 300 to 200 titles, but still includes readings from the Bible. Texas Braces for Severe Weather Gov. Greg Abbott has activated ahead of potentially severe weather over the weekend and into next week. Light rain and wet conditions are expected this morning through many parts of the state, with this evening and risk of hail, flash flooding, and tornadoes rising in some areas tomorrow. The National Weather Service is advising that conditions may persist , with chances of strong to severe storms in the afternoon and evenings each day. In his statement Friday, Abbott asked Texans to make a and follow guidance from local officials. | No Cord. No Hassle. Just Pure Cleaning Power. () ➤ Houston: A teen riding his bike in the Addicks Reservoir discovered the body of a woman thought to be between 30 and 40 years old. Police are investigating her death as a homicide. () ➤ Galveston: Royal Caribbean announced Thursday that its cruise liner Symphony of the Seas will be repositioned from Galveston to Fort Lauderdale, Fla. for the 2027 season. () ➤ Tyler: Invasive hammerhead worms have re-emerged in East Texas after heavy rains hit the region. The worms prey on critical soil decomposers like earthworms and are extremely difficult to kill. () ➤ New Braunfels: The city has entered the final phase of a $7 million wastewater renovation project in its Historic Gruene District. The project has disrupted traffic patterns for months in one of the city's most-visited areas. () ➤ Weslaco: The family of Larissa Rodriguez, who died suddenly at 17 due in part to "large amounts of caffeine," has filed a wrongful death lawsuit against the Glazer's Beer and Beverage, the distributor of Alani Nu energy drinks, claiming the drink "had inadequate warnings about the serious cardiac risks." subject: $500 Million Supplemental Budget Signed by Gov. Janet Mills After Partisan Passage
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** $500 Million Supplemental Budget Signed by Gov. Janet Mills After Partisan Passage (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=08d5674d66&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Libby Palanza on Apr 10, 2026 06:43 pm
Maine’s $500 million supplemental budget was signed into law by Gov. Janet Mills (D) Friday after narrowly passing the House and Senate the day before. While lawmakers in the House were divided along strict partisan lines, one Democrat in the Senate, Sen. Nicole Grohoski (D-Hancock), broke ranks to join the Republicans in opposition to the [...]

clean_body: Where wickedness lurks, God builds up warriors to destroy it. | | | | | | Friend, | | The Scriptures remind us that when the RIGHTEOUS are in authority, the people rejoice. But when the wicked rule, the people groan. Right now, our nation is groaning under the weight of policies that ignore God 's law and seek to redefine reality itself. Former Army Green Beret John Frankman isn't running for Congress to be a politician. He's running in FL-01 to SERVE as a steward of God 's blessings that our ancestors FOUGHT to secure for us. | | | | | We need leaders who more than they fear the left-wing media, the foreign lobbyists, or the radical elites who pull the strings. If you are SICK & TIRED of the status quo and ready to stand for our future, Your support will allow us to spread the LORD's message that America remains a land built on Christian principles - and we intend to for ourselves & our posterity. | | | | | Thank you for joining our fight to restore FAITH & FREEDOM in our nation's capital. For God's Country, Frankman HQ | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | Pop Acta Media | Republican Task Force PO Box 461 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

subject: Today: Super Saturday

body:
# [](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/629?refcode=em20260411-487&t=1&akid=487%2E12592%2ET2miOn)
Today is the day.
Democrats across Virginia are preparing to head out the door and start talking to their neighbors to protect our elections.
[**Before we walk out the door, can you rush a donation of $3 to help us keep our Super Saturday running smoothly? We’ve had a great turnout of volunteers, and we need to make sure that they all have the supplies that they need today.**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260411_1.k2m70F&amount=3&refcode=20260411-487&t=2&akid=487%2E12592%2ET2miOn)

Hundreds of volunteers will be knocking doors, having real conversations, and making sure voters understand what’s at stake before April 21.
MAGA Republicans are already pouring money into Virginia to drown out the truth. We’re responding the only way that works. Talking to people, neighbor to neighbor.
If you can’t be out there with us today, you can still be part of this effort.
[**Will you chip in $3 right now to help us equip our volunteers with clipboards, literature, and the tools they need to reach voters?**](https://act.virginiansforfairelections.com/go/41?c0=em_ms_vafe_20260411_1.k2m70F&amount=3&refcode=20260411-487&t=4&akid=487%2E12592%2ET2miOn)

We’re all in for these final days.
Thank you for being part of this,
Team Virginians For Fair Elections

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: AI and the Liberty Movement
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/msmomentum-12852787?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend,
Amazon has just announced another $12 billion investment in Mississippi. It is genuinely great news — and just the latest in a flood of inward investment into our state in the past couple of years.

But here is the thing worth reflecting on: even AI-related investments on this scale are a fraction of what is flowing into datacenters and AI infrastructure across the country. **What is happening in AI is not just another tech cycle. It is going to be absolutely massive — and genuinely transformative in ways that will touch every kind of institution.**

You would not know that from much of the media coverage. I’ve lost count of the number of articles warning about catastrophic job losses, mass unemployment, and whole industries being wiped out. **The narrative has been relentlessly negative. But I think it is wrong — and I am not alone in thinking so.**

Marc Andreessen, one of the sharpest minds in technology, put it plainly in a recent post:
---
|
---
| I think he is right. And a conversation at our recent event in Jackson with Matt Ridley helped crystallize in my mind as to why.

**Matt Ridley told our audience about something called the Jevons paradox.** Named after the nineteenth-century economist William Stanley Jevons, the idea is counterintuitive but well-established: when something becomes cheaper or more efficient to use, people do not simply consume less of it — they consume more. **Efficiency generates demand rather than redundancy.**

So, too, with AI. Yes, it may make legal advice or specialist expertise far more affordable and widely available. But that does not mean lawyers and professionals will be put out of work. It means people will seek legal advice far more often than they did when access was expensive and limited. **Making intelligence radically cheaper will not make smart people redundant. It will unleash more of it.**

**We are already beginning to see the first signs of an AI productivity boom.** I am convinced that the people and organizations that embrace AI and use it effectively will not simply do the same things faster. They will become hyper-productive — able to produce, communicate, research, and act at a level that was simply not possible before. That is not a recipe for fewer jobs. It is a recipe for more output, more value, and more opportunity.

**Running a think tank, I find myself thinking a great deal about what AI means for organizations like MCPP.** Over the past forty years, the liberty movement in America has built an impressive infrastructure — dozens of policy institutes in Washington and one in virtually every state. That network has done enormous good. But there is a challenge that comes with maturity and growth.

As organizations get larger, productivity per person can fall. What begins as a lean, mission-driven operation can gradually become more bureaucratic. The original focus blurs. Without constant effort to guard against it, there is a real danger that — rather like a rain dancer claiming credit for precipitation — organizations end up claiming agency for things that would have happened anyway.

**AI, I believe, is about to reenergize the liberty movement in all sorts of wonderful ways.** It might even, whisper it softly, be a little disruptive — in the best possible sense.

**Small organizations that use AI well can now be more effective than much larger ones that do not.** The capacity of a campaign group should never be measured by the size of its payroll — and AI is only going to make that point blindingly obvious.

Here at MCPP, we have started to use AI in lots of different creative ways. We are working on our first animated children’s cartoon, based on our children’s book _What Makes America Special_. We have enormous amounts of data, and AI now allows us to use it in smarter ways to identify and reach exactly the people we need to be talking to. MCPP already has perhaps the largest owned audience of any conservative organization in this state — and AI means we are experimenting with new ways to take that our communication reach even further.

**AI will never replace the personal relationships that sit at the heart of public policy work.** But what it can do is free us up to spend more time on exactly those human connections — the conversations, the trust-building, the relationships with legislators and opinion-formers that no algorithm will ever replicate.

The multi-billion dollar data-centers now dotted across states like Mississippi are only one of the ways in which the AI revolution is making itself felt here. **The deeper transformation — the one that will reshape how organizations like ours think, communicate, and campaign — is only just beginning. We intend to be at the front of it.**
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=8867ba0cea&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warm regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO
---
|
---
| | _ _

Mississippi Center for Public Policy520 George StJackson, MS 39202-3013

You can [update your preferences](https://mcpp-c58f.mailchimpsites.com/manage/preferences?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&e=5703353baa&c=768ca080f5) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=2f68021ebd&t=b&e=5703353baa&c=768ca080f5).

name: Mark Kelly
subject: North Carolina and Ohio
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
body:
#

Friend, if you knew chipping in $10 could win a majority in the Senate, wouldn’t you chip in right away?
If you knew that donation meant a Democratic majority in the Senate could finally take real action and rein in Trump’s abuses of power, wouldn’t that be some of the best money you ever spent?
Imagine all this chaos today, and then imagine if we held the levers of power in Congress.
I’m not saying one donation is going to do all of that, but your donation in the right place at the right time can have a huge impact. And when thousands of other people chip in alongside you, that’s how we start to win.
So, look. I’m not writing to you for myself today. I’m asking you to step up and make a donation to two of my friends: Sherrod Brown and Roy Cooper.
If we’re going to have any chance at a Senate majority, we need to get Sherrod and Roy elected this November.
[**And if that’s worth $10 to you, please: Split that donation between Sherrod and Roy and give them a fighting chance at winning two of the toughest races in the entire country this year.**](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1389?c0=mek-cooper-brown-2026.sU-zPA&refcode=em260411&akid=6410%2E6688874%2Eut2Hvm -90&t=1)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly between Roy Cooper and Sherrod Brown:_
| | **[CHIP IN $10](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=mek-cooper-brown-2026.RoYlTj&amount=5&refcode=em260411&akid=6410%2E6688874%2Eut2Hvm -90&t=2)**
---
****
| **[CHIP IN MORE](https://act.markkelly.com/go/1395?c0=mek-cooper-brown-2026.RoYlTj&refcode=em260411&akid=6410%2E6688874%2Eut2Hvm -90&t=3)**
---
****
Thank you.
Mark

Paid For By Mark Kelly for Senate
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here](https://act.markkelly.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=4&akid=6410%2E6688874%2Eut2Hvm).
Mark Kelly was a Captain in the U.S. Navy and astronaut. Use of his military rank, job titles and photographs in uniform does not imply endorsement by the Department of the Navy or Department of Defense and reference to NASA does not imply endorsement by the National Aeronautics and Space Administration.
